subtopics.title: The Day of Pentecost
common_misconceptions: Speaking in tongues means speaking gibberish (in Acts, it was real languages)
Pentecost is only about speaking in tongues (it's about empowerment for witness)
The Church started before Pentecost (officially born at Pentecost)
subtopics.teaching_note: Acts 2: Pentecost (50 days after Passover/Resurrection). Symbols: wind (spirit's power), tongues of fire (purification), speaking in tongues (ability to witness to all nations). Peter's sermon: quotes Joel 2 (prophecy fulfilled), Psalm 16 (David spoke of resurrection), Psalm 110 (Lord said to my Lord). 3000 baptized. Significance: universal church born, Holy Spirit indwells believers, gospel for all nations. Nigerian context: Pentecostal movement is very strong - Deeper Life, Redeemed Christian Church of God, Living Faith (Winners), Mountain of Fire.

subtopics.title: The Three Missionary Journeys
common_misconceptions: Paul was one of the original twelve (he was an apostle but not one of the twelve)
Paul's conversion happened immediately on the road (he was blinded then, baptized later in Damascus)
Paul always traveled alone (had various companions)
subtopics.teaching_note: Paul (formerly Saul): persecuted Christians → converted on Damascus road (Acts 9). Missionary journeys: 1st (Acts 13-14): Cyprus, Pamphylia, Galatia with Barnabas. 2nd (Acts 15-18): through Asia Minor to Macedonia (Philippi, Thessalonica, Berea, Athens, Corinth) with Silas. 3rd (Acts 18-21): strengthened churches in Galatia and Phrygia, long stay in Ephesus, collection for Jerusalem. Key events: stoning at Lystra, Philippian jail, Areopagus sermon in Athens, riot at Ephesus. Letters written during journeys: Galatians, 1&2 Thessalonians, 1&2 Corinthians, Romans.

subtopics.title: Romans and Corinthians
common_misconceptions: Paul taught that works don't matter (he taught works don't save, but should follow salvation)
1 Corinthians 13 is only for weddings (it's about all Christian relationships)
2 Corinthians is just about money (it's about authentic ministry)
subtopics.teaching_note: Romans: systematic theology. Justification by faith alone (Rom 1:17, 3:28). All have sinned (3:23). Salvation by grace through faith (Eph 2:8-9, elaborated in Romans). 1 Corinthians: problems in Corinth church - divisions, immorality, lawsuits, marriage, food sacrificed to idols, worship (ch 11-14), resurrection (ch 15). 1 Cor 13: love chapter - love is patient, kind, not envious, not proud. 2 Corinthians: Paul's defense of his apostleship, ministry of reconciliation (5:17-21), collection for Jerusalem.
